Explain how an mRNA vaccine teaches the immune system.?
The mRNA vaccine teaches the immune system to recognize the virus as a threat. The immune system then produces antibodies to fight the virus. Include the following in your description: the role of RNA polymerase, the role of the promoter, and the role of the terminator.
Describe how DNA is transcribed into RNA in a cell. Include the following in your description: the role of RNA polymerase, the role of the promoter, and the role of the terminator.
Transcription is the process by which the information in a gene, encoded as a sequence of DNA nucleotides, is copied into a complementary sequence of RNA nucleotides. The process of transcription is similar to DNA replication, but differs in several key ways. Transcription takes place in the nucleus, while DNA replication takes place in the cytoplasm.
